creator: tcberner@freebsd.org
text:
(In reply to Gleb Popov from comment #4)
I think getting to use twm is warning enough :P

Adding a message would force people who are happy with the default session (let's assume such a person exist) force to create their own xinitrc too.
So, I'm not sure whether that is a good thing.

But I think fixing sddm's session-script and adding a pkg-message to sddm might at least serve as enough documentation -- or while were looking at documentation: https://docs.freebsd.org/en/books/handbook/x11/#x11-wm should be updated to say "hey, if you want to use sddm's-xssesion, make sure to have an ~/.xinitrc"


mfg Tobias
